g.text: Restoration in the Presence of Noise Only

text.data-narration: Image restoration is a crucial task in computer vision that involves removing degradations from an image to restore its original state.

g.text: Image restoration is a crucial task in computer vision that involves removing degradations from an image to restore its original state.

text.data-narration: Random fluctuations in pixel values can be represented by a probability distribution.

g.text: Random fluctuations in pixel values can be represented by a probability distribution.

text.data-narration: Gaussian noise has a Gaussian distribution and can be represented by a probability distribution.

animate.text: Gaussian noise has a Gaussian distribution and can be represented by a probability distribution.

text.data-narration: Additive noise is a type of noise that is added to the original image.

g.text: Additive noise is a type of noise that is added to the original image.

text.data-narration: Multiplicative noise is a type of noise that is multiplied with the original image.

animate.text: Multiplicative noise is a type of noise that is multiplied with the original image.

text.data-narration: The mathematical model of image restoration can be represented by the following equation: I(x) = f(x) + n(x) where I(x) is the restored image, f(x) is the original image, and n(x) is the noise.

g.text: The mathematical model of image restoration can be represented by the following equation: I(x) = f(x) + n(x) where I(x) is the restored image, f(x) is the original image, and n(x) is the noise.

text.data-narration: The mathematical model of image restoration can be represented by the following equation: I(x) = f(x) + n(x) where I(x) is the restored image, f(x) is the original image, and n(x) is the noise.

animate.text: The mathematical model of image restoration can be represented by the following equation: I(x) = f(x) + n(x) where I(x) is the restored image, f(x) is the original image, and n(x) is the noise.

text.data-narration: Filtering is one of the most popular denoising techniques that involves applying a filter to the image to remove the noise. Filtering can be categorized into two types: additive and multiplicative filtering.

g.text: Filtering is one of the most popular denoising techniques that involves applying a filter to the image to remove the noise. Filtering can be categorized into two types: additive and multiplicative filtering.

text.data-narration: Low-pass filtering removes high-frequency components of the image, while high-pass filtering removes low-frequency components of the image.

animate.text: Low-pass filtering removes high-frequency components of the image, while high-pass filtering removes low-frequency components of the image.

text.data-narration: Statistical model-based methods use probability distributions to model the noise and restore the image. Bayesian estimation is a popular method that uses Bayes' theorem to estimate the probability distribution of the noise.

g.text: Statistical model-based methods use probability distributions to model the noise and restore the image. Bayesian estimation is a popular method that uses Bayes' theorem to estimate the probability distribution of the noise.

text.data-narration: Bayesian estimation is a popular method that uses Bayes' theorem to estimate the probability distribution of the noise.

animate.text: Bayesian estimation is a popular method that uses Bayes' theorem to estimate the probability distribution of the noise.

g.text: Thank you for learning about Restoration in the Presence of Noise Only!
